feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to allocate Capacity Units (CU) to SAC user licenses
entry.content:
We are currently experiencing a shortage of SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) user licenses.
After consulting with SAP in advance, we were informed that we need to allocate our contracted Capacity Units (CU) to SAC user licenses.

Could you please advise us on the specific procedure for allocating CU to SAC user licenses and the necessary steps to take?

entry.title: Updating property of a dimension via another property
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a requirement where I have to update property(Flag) of Cost Object dimension. This Flag column needs to be updated by L2 property of the same dimension. So, where L2='A' the flag should be 'Yes'.

The issue is, the L2 for which flag is Yes will be given by user via story and then running some data action, flag should be updated. Since, both are properties, data action is not working.I have tried creating another model keeping L2 and Flag and then joining it with main model. This is also not working in DATA function as we are trying to use 2 properties in it.

Any other way to solve this

Thanks.

entry.title: Impossible to copy/paste an object, from one page of a report to another, or recreate it
entry.content:
I have a report with 3 pages. From the 2nd page, I need to copy some of the scripting code lines and objects, to a 3rd recently created page. When finished, the 3rd page will replace the 2nd one.
I can copy/paste the code lines, but not all the objects (those referenced in the script). 
For only one of them (of type "calculated input control"), it's impossible for me to copy/paste this object, or even simply recreate it into the 3rd page. This object can be used as it is, in the 2nd page.

Could you please explain me why ? If this object is no more available in our used version (2026.02), what is replacing it, and how could I replace it correctly ?

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Icons are not visible while changing the role
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

Our team has decided to adjust roles so that everyone only sees what they should.

We have created a new test team with a role called 'BI Content View', where you can only view data and cannot modify it etc. I think it is clear (In secutiry --- Users --- Teams/Roles/Users)

Everything is working perfectly: Scripting works fine (users can click on buttons and will be redirected, etc.), but the icons are no longer visible! For example, the '!' icon or other icons near the buttons.

You can see this in the screenshots below. Two variants with Admin-Role and Viewer-Role

Admin:

Viewer:

 

I tried adjusting the roles by adding custom widgets (Read, etc.), but it didn't help. Has anyone had this issue before and know which option needs adjusting?

entry.title: SAC setVisible via Switch
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am currently trying to set up my first true Story in SAC. I am using a live model with given Measures. I'd like to have a table that can dynamically change. Since I couldn't figure out how to change the used Measures and Dimensions in a table I tried setting up two seperate tables and toggleing their visibility with a switch. I tried various ways to make it work but since my programming knowladge is pretty low I just could not get it to run as I'd like to.

 Here you can see my code: 

var isSwitch2On = this.isOn();

if (isSwitch2On) {
		YTD_Tabelle.setVisible(false);
        Month_Tabelle.setVisible(true);
    } else {
        YTD_Tabelle.setVisible(false);
		Month_Tabelle.setVisible(true);
	}

I do not get any red likes or somthing and every variable is surely written correctly. However when I try to use the switch in view mode I always recieve a message simmilar to this one:

 

(Script execution failed for event "Switch_2.onChange" Correlation ID: 9553790471024494b717229046088729)

In the styling options I set the "View time visibility" to hidden for the Month_Tabelle and to visible for the YTD_Tabelle.

Thank you everyone for your help and time! 🙂

entry.title: SAC Story embedded in Fiori LP - Jump to... not working for hierarchies
entry.content:
Hi All,

we are trying to setup SAC Stories based on S4 CDS views. For us it is crucial that the SAC Stories are embedded in Fiori Launchpad and that the integration into other Fiori Apps as e.g. Actual Line Items works smoothly. We have a SAC Story embedded, but the G/L Account and Cost Center hierarchies are not resolved as soon as we jump into a Line Item Fiori App.

Display Line Items in General Ledger window is empty.
Anyone has a solution for that?

entry.title: SAC Scripting Function Top N Filter
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

for my story I implemented a dynamic measure change via a switch for three of my charts. One of them is as standard equipped with a top n filter. However, when i flip the switch and the new measures are shown the filter is being removed.

Do you know if there is a function to enable the filter again afterwards?

Thank you all in advance!

var measuresStandard2 = ["", "", "", ""];
var measuresAlternative2 = ["", "", "", ""];

var measuresStandard3 = [""];
var measuresAlternative3 = [""];

var isSwitchOn = this.isOn();

if (isSwitchOn) {
    // Chart 1
    for (var i = 0; i < measuresStandard1.length; i++) { Chart_1.removeMeasure(measuresStandard1[i], Feed.ValueAxis); }
    for (var j = 0; j < measuresAlternative1.length; j++) { Chart_1.addMeasure(measuresAlternative1[j], Feed.ValueAxis); }

    // Chart 2
    for (var k = 0; k < measuresStandard2.length; k++) { Chart_2.removeMeasure(measuresStandard2[k], Feed.ValueAxis); }
    for (var l = 0; l < measuresAlternative2.length; l++) { Chart_2.addMeasure(measuresAlternative2[l], Feed.ValueAxis); }

    // Chart 3
    for (var m3 = 0; m3 < measuresStandard3.length; m3++) { Chart_3.removeMeasure(measuresStandard3[m3], Feed.ValueAxis); }
    for (var n3 = 0; n3 < measuresAlternative3.length; n3++) { Chart_3.addMeasure(measuresAlternative3[n3], Feed.ValueAxis); }

} else {
    // Chart 1
    for (var m = 0; m < measuresAlternative1.length; m++) { Chart_1.removeMeasure(measuresAlternative1[m], Feed.ValueAxis); }
    for (var n = 0; n < measuresStandard1.length; n++) { Chart_1.addMeasure(measuresStandard1[n], Feed.ValueAxis); }

    // Chart 2
    for (var o = 0; o < measuresAlternative2.length; o++) { Chart_2.removeMeasure(measuresAlternative2[o], Feed.ValueAxis); }
    for (var p = 0; p < measuresStandard2.length; p++) { Chart_2.addMeasure(measuresStandard2[p], Feed.ValueAxis); }

    // Chart 3
    for (var o3 = 0; o3 < measuresAlternative3.length; o3++) { Chart_3.removeMeasure(measuresAlternative3[o3], Feed.ValueAxis); }
    for (var p3 = 0; p3 < measuresStandard3.length; p3++) { Chart_3.addMeasure(measuresStandard3[p3], Feed.ValueAxis); }
}

entry.title: SAC: Scoping the date dimension using source version and target version property
entry.content:
Hi all, 

Background: M creating a data action to copy data from a source version to a target version (both are members of version dimension having different start date and end date properties). I need to use advanced formula because there are multiple conditions which are achieved. However, the performance of the data action is slow due to not scoping date dimension.

Issue: I need help to restrict the date dimension in the config part itself such that, the dates between the start date of the source version and end date of the target version are only processed further.

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: SAC Data Blending issue
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am experiencing a data duplication issue when exporting
a blended data model from SAC Stories to Excel/CSV.

ENVIRONMENT:
- SAP Analytics Cloud (Stories)
- Live SuccessFactors EC connection
- Data Blending between 2 models
- Both models are flat compensation portlets

ISSUE:
When exporting blended table via widget Export,
rows are duplicated in multiples of 20 (x20, x40, etc)

PATTERN OBSERVED:
- Under 10,000 rows → export is clean, no duplication
- Over 20,000 rows → duplication starts, always x20 pattern
- SAC table/story displays correctly, issue only on export
- Duplication happens with composite key blend only
- Personnel Number only blend works fine at 80,000+ rows

COMPOSITE KEY FORMULA:
PersonnelNumber + PayComponentID +
YEAR + zero-padded MONTH + zero-padded DAY + Sequence

WHAT I HAVE TRIED:
- Added zero padding to month and day in key formula
- Verified no duplicates exist in source models individually
- Verified key uniqueness in both models
- Tested with simple flat portlets (no self-join) - same issue
- Widget export and general export both produce duplicates

CONCLUSION:
Issue is reproducible and consistent. Appears to be SAC
export pagination bug specific to composite key blending
on large datasets with live SuccessFactors connection.

Has anyone experienced this? Is there a known workaround
or SAP note addressing this?

Thank you

entry.title: Date Filter Resets to System Date Instead of Custom Date Input
entry.content:
I have defined a custom date input control. I am using this date input control in the filter section of a custom widget.

The problem is that the date filter always switches back to the system date instead of using the custom date defined in the date input control. This behavior does not occur with restricted key figures; it only happens in the filter section of the chart.

It appears to be a general issue with custom widgets.

entry.title: SAC Data Action for Allocation
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Could you please assist in developing the Data Action logic for the following requirement?

Dimension: ALLOC_CYCLE
This dimension contains the required properties.

ID

Stage

Active

Driver

Parenth

AC_CYCLE1

1

Y

D_DRIVER1

ALL_AC

AC_CYCLE2

2

N

D_DRIVER2

ALL_AC

AC_CYCLE3

6

Y

D_DRIVER3

ALL_AC

AC_CYCLE4

8

Y

D_DRIVER1

ALL_AC

AC_CYCLE5

5

Y

D_DRIVER2

ALL_AC

Data Action Parameters:

  • Allocation_Cycle
  • Target Version (Default)

The user can either select a single allocation cycle or choose a hierarchy to process multiple cycles.

Validation Rules for Allocation

  1. For the selected allocation cycle(s), the system should validate whether the Cycle Active flag = "Y". If the cycle is not active, it should be excluded from processing.
  2. Allocation cycles should be processed based on their Stage, in ascending order (i.e., from Stage "1" through Stage "N").

Logic for Allocation

  1. Reading of Drivers Data  - Driver data is stored under:
    • Allocation Cycle = "NO_CYCLE"
    • Account = CYCLE.DRIVERS (property of Allocation Cycle dimension)

    •  

       

      Date

      P01 (2026)

      P02 (2026)

      P03 (2026)

      P04 (2026)

      P05 (2026)

      P06 (2026)

      P07 (2026)

      P08 (2026)

      P09 (2026)

      P10 (2026)

      P11 (2026)

      P12 (2026)

      Account

      Allocation Cycle

      Costobject

       

       

       

       

       

       

       

       

       

       

       

       

      D_DRIVER1

      NO_CYCLE

      YZETLYJGKC

      0.007788

      0.855474

      0.084999

      0.081403

      0.529231

      0.013347

      0.562226

      0.05978

      0.039798

      0.179218

      0.696424

      0.914806

      D_DRIVER1

      NO_CYCLE

      XPUCXNVFHA

      0.959009

      0.891055

      0.898892

      0.616775

      0.233873

      0.668626

      0.890584

      0.993324

      0.146145

      0.780597

      0.93994

      0.158102

      D_DRIVER1

      NO_CYCLE

      TMRJJUDRYC

      0.218391

      0.15194

      0.139433

      0.929238

      0.946945

      0.27212

      0.204468

      0.588206

      0.71644

      0.391042

      0.897587

      D_DRIVER1

      NO_CYCLE

      YLULXTHXZK

      0.040966

      0.153406

      0.270626

      0.199465

      0.15652

      0.190965

      0.907461

      0.650826

      0.225456

      0.269511

      0.225198

      0.662109

      D_DRIVER2

      NO_CYCLE

      YZETLYJGKC

      0.98477

      0.018141

      0.792378

      0.790975

      0.245779

      0.716081

      0.290322

      0.460564

      XPUCXNVFHA

      0.491832

      0.808881

      0.542514

      0.350384

      0.088891

      0.104948

      0.578834

      0.278451

      0.567371

      0.897166

      0.086918

      D_DRIVER2

      NO_CYCLE

      TMRJJUDRYC

      0.371288

      0.761732

      0.526606

      0.001278

      0.63877

      0.387049

      YLULXTHXZK

      0.940818

      D_DRIVER3

      YZETLYJGKC

      0.176089

      D_DRIVER3

      XPUCXNVFHA

      0.332141

      0.185871

      NO_CYCLE

      TMRJJUDRYC

      0.275606

      0.164455

      D_DRIVER4

      NO_CYCLE

      XPUCXNVFHA

      0.936303

      0.580602

      0.666231

      0.246926

      0.334287

      0.446345

      D_DRIVER4

      NO_CYCLE

      TMRJJUDRYC

      0.612572

      0.842846

      0.254031

      0.936815

      0.083911

      0.786158

      0.817653

      D_DRIVER4

      NO_CYCLE

      YLULXTHXZK

      0.789067

      0.89359

      0.493027

      0.277655

      0.746934

      0.359162

      0.477477

      0.714554

      D_DRIVER5

      NO_CYCLE

      YZETLYJGKC

      0.479361

      0.940726

      0.221336

      0.183893

      0.774026

      0.081278

      0.999697

      0.303012

      0.143791

      0.166389

      0.518606

      0.066061

      D_DRIVER5

      NO_CYCLE

      XPUCXNVFHA

      0.526565

      0.719342

      0.540826

      0.936581

      0.618076

      D_DRIVER5

      NO_CYCLE

      TMRJJUDRYC

      0.199597

      0.372548

      0.408633

      0.207466

      0.443836

      0.09373

      0.188007

      0.365207

      0.724277

      0.941259

      0.556049

      0.830857

      D_DRIVER5

      NO_CYCLE

      YLULXTHXZK

      0.155097

      0.571414

      0.938727

      0.843546

      0.792186

      0.927327

      0.034974

      0.779268

      0.432636

      0.257151

      0.262203

      0.714602

    • Example:
      If the user selects Allocation Cycle = AC_CYCLE4, its corresponding driver data is stored under Account = DRIVER2.

      Requirement:
      Retrieve driver data for each selected allocation cycle and aggregate/summarize it by:

      • Allocation Cycle
      • Account
      • Time (excluding Cost Object)

     

  2. Reading of Non Allocated Data -  Below filter to read non allocated data >

                Filter - Allocation Cycle ≠ Current Processing Cycle

 

 

Date

P01 (2026)

P02 (2026)

P03 (2026)

P04 (2026)

P05 (2026)

P06 (2026)

P07 (2026)

P08 (2026)

P09 (2026)

P10 (2026)

P11 (2026)

P12 (2026)

Account

Allocation Cycle

Costobject

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

100001

AC_CYCLE1

YZETLYJGKC

389856

868585

508690

404820

35911

62869

880594

681904

195872

914810

128550

116960

100002

AC_CYCLE3

XPUCXNVFHA

731502

972995

162733

493933

489458

828327

857149

948476

406478

976030

970460

180498

AC_CYCLE5

TMRJJUDRYC

272880

708077

395376

302071

948950

227214

100004

AC_CYCLE4

YLULXTHXZK

913469

233813

664615

672946

Example:
If AC_CYCLE4 is being processed, its data should be excluded from the source dataset.

In this case system should not pick “AC_CYCLE4” data as we passed this as a parameter selection

3. Allocation Processing

  • Loop through Non-Allocated Data
  • Loop through Driver Data
  • Use the aggregated driver values to perform allocation calculations
  • Each record from Non-Allocated Data should be processed against all relevant driver records for the respective allocation cycle.
  1. Posting Results
  • Write back the calculated allocation data to the target version.

@N1kh1l  - Could you please help on this.

 

Best regards,

Aastha

entry.title: Can We Build Live Insights Using CDS Views and SAP Analytics Cloud Within 15 Days?
entry.content:
Hello Community,

My name is E Pranith Kumar, and I am a fresher. My team and I are eager to take up a meaningful challenge and deliver a strong outcome within a short timeline.

We are exploring the possibility of building live insights using CDS Views and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). I would like to understand:

  • Is it feasible to build live analytical insights using CDS Views and SAC within 15 days?
  • If yes, what are the key prerequisites (system setup, authorizations, versions, and connectivity)?
  • Which T-codes/tools are typically used for:
    • Creating and exposing CDS Views
    • Enabling live connectivity to SAC
    • Authorization and data access

We have been provided with a few use cases (attached below). Since we are new to this domain, your guidance would be extremely helpful:

  • Which use case would be best suited for a short timeline?
  • Which one offers the best feature scope and business impact?
  • What SAP modules are typically involved for the recommended use case?
  • What type of system access or roles would be required to implement it?

Additionally, if possible, could you please recommend any learning resources or documentation that would help us research, learn, and implement this effectively?

Although we are new to CDS Views and SAC, we are highly motivated and ready to put in the effort. With your guidance, we are confident we can deliver meaningful insights.

Thank you very much for your time and support. I truly appreciate any direction you can provide.

Best regards,
E Pranith Kumar

entry.title: Effectivecontext not capturing all updated lines in single entry/fluid entry mode
entry.content:
Hi,

We have used onafterdataentryprocess event and capturing only changed cost object-cost element combination in effectiveContext.These are getting passed in multi action as parameters.

Issue is when we are making changes in multiple lines in the table in single entry mode/fluid entry mode, it is taking only last updated line. This effectiveContext is only working when we are using Mass entry mode.

Is this a known limitation with single entry/fluid entry mode.

Thanks.

entry.title: Calculate Average Salary per Job level using data action
entry.content:
Dear SAC Experts,

I am trying to calculate Average Salary per Job level using Advanced Formulas in Data action. Below is the sample data and I need to calculate Average Salary at Job level. I am not able to get the count value populated. 

Below is the code:

In the next step while writing data, I am calculating Average by using (@totalsalary/@count).

Could anyone of you please help with the code.

entry.title: Dimension Hierarchy Text gets truncated in SAC stories
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a SAC story that uses dimension hierarchies in widgets. The text of the hierarchy node in those widgets is getting truncated. In the SAP BW, the hierarchy is maintained correctly. But when used in SAC stories, it gets truncated.  The size of the widget does not impact; even with larger widgets, the text does not display correctly. 

Please let me know how we can resolve this.

Regards,

Pratik Shah

entry.title: How Actual and planning data cab be reported combinedly in SAC?
entry.content:
HI Team,

Could you please check and share how we can achieve below scenario in SAP Data Sphere and SAC?

Lets say, I have Sales data in data sphere tables which is actuals and wanted to load this data to SAC for planning. After planning, I want to report actuals and plan in same report in SAC. 

1. Can we store actuals also in SAC along with planning? Is it a good approach?

2. Should I access actual data from data sphere to SAC using live connection and prepare the plan data in SAC. In this case, is plan data stores in SAC or shall I push the plan data also to Data sphere to do the combine reporting from Data sphere to SAC using live connection.

3. Or do we need to maintain the actuals in Data sphere and plan data in SAC, and do the reporting using some views or models in SAC.

Which is the best approach to do the actuals and planning data in same report in SAC. Kindly share your feedback.

entry.title: Mac user is unable to use the SAC Excel Add-in on the desktop or web
entry.content: Mac user is unable to use the SAC Excel Add-in on the desktop or web. He is not able to access the Admin Managed section in the Add-in icon

entry.title: SAC: Data not refreshed by refresh button/auto refresh - No "Live Data" possible?
entry.content:
Dear all,

 

We are facing the following problem:
Data in SAC dashboard are not refreshed when using the standard refresh button or scripts like Application.refreshData().
We need to reopen the browser page in order to get the latest set of data. 

Setup: 
Dashboard -> CDS VM -> S/4HANA Tables

Caching is already disabled in RSRT: 3384063 - Refresh button from SAP Analytics Cloud Story does not update the data for BW live data connection - SAP for Me

May I ask you for any ideas how real "Live Data" could be achieved? 
In the final solution we would like to use the auto refresh function in SAC with an interval of a few minutes.

Thank you

entry.title: SAC Story - Tab Strip Builder not working
entry.content:
Dear SAC Experts,

When I started building my SAC Story, I inserted a Tab Strip on 2 of the pages. The project was put on hold for a couple of months, but today I revisited it to continue building on it, only to find that the builder tab for only one of the tab strips was working. The other on was simply just returning a blank page.

Have any of you ever run into that problem as well? And if yes, do you have a solution?

Thanks in Advance!

entry.title: YTD Function in Calculated Measure of Model Doesn't Work
entry.content:
I'm trying to create a calculated measure in my model using the YTD function.  (The measure is at the model level, not the story level.)  The formula I'm using is this:  YTD([MTD], [d/Date]).

When I test the measure in a story, no results display.  I am simply pulling GL Account in the rows and Date in the columns.  YTD is the measure.  Why are no results displaying?
